21.correct: Antonie van Leeuwenhoek is known as the 'Father of Microbiology' for being the first to observe microorganisms using his microscopes.

wrong.B: Louis Pasteur is the father of modern microbiology/germ theory, but the 'father of microbiology' title here is van Leeuwenhoek.

21.fact: Van Leeuwenhoek called the tiny organisms he saw 'animalcules'.

22.correct: Halons are ozone-depleting substances that release bromine atoms when exposed to UV radiation in the stratosphere.
22.fact: Bromine is far more destructive to ozone, per atom, than chlorine.

23.correct: Hydrochlorofluorocarbons (HCFCs) are ozone-depleting substances that release chlorine on exposure to UV radiation.

wrong.A: Methyl bromide releases bromine, not iodine.
wrong.B: Carbon tetrachloride releases chlorine, but the correctly matched pair here is HCFC-chlorine.

23.fact: The Montreal Protocol phases out ozone-depleting substances like CFCs and HCFCs.

24.correct: The forebrain (cerebrum) is mainly responsible for thinking, reasoning and intelligence.

wrong.C: The hindbrain controls balance and vital functions.
wrong.D: The spinal cord conducts impulses and reflexes.

24.fact: The cerebrum is the largest part of the human brain and the seat of higher thought.

25.correct: In June 2025, Telangana MLA T. Raja Singh resigned, citing dissatisfaction with the state leadership.

wrong.A: Bandi Sanjay did not resign here.
wrong.C: Kishan Reddy is a Union Minister.
wrong.D: Etela Rajender is not the one who resigned in this instance.

25.fact: MLAs are members of a state Legislative Assembly.

26.correct: In September 2024, Udhayanidhi Stalin was appointed Deputy Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu.

wrong.B: K. Palaniswami is the AIADMK leader/opposition.
wrong.C: O. Panneerselvam is not the Deputy CM here.
wrong.D: V. Senthil Balaji is a minister, not the Deputy CM.

26.fact: Udhayanidhi Stalin is the son of Tamil Nadu CM M.K. Stalin.

27.correct: Both statements are correct - the kidneys lie on either side of the backbone in the abdomen (I), and urine produced in the kidneys passes through the ureters to the bladder (II).

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are correct.

27.fact: The human excretory system includes two kidneys, two ureters, a bladder and a urethra.

28.correct: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change notified the Eco-mark Rules in September 2024.
28.fact: The Eco-mark scheme labels environment-friendly consumer products.

29.correct: Earth is the fifth largest planet in our solar system (after Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune).

wrong.B: Venus is slightly smaller than Earth.
wrong.D: Uranus is larger than Earth (third or fourth largest).

29.fact: By size the order is Jupiter > Saturn > Uranus > Neptune > Earth.

30.correct: The Nadaswaram (also called 'Nagaswaram') is an indigenous wind instrument, among the loudest non-brass acoustic instruments.

wrong.A: The Gogona is a jaw-harp of Assam.
wrong.B: The Timki is a small drum.
wrong.C: The harmonium is a keyboard wind instrument, not 'Nayanam'.

30.fact: The Nadaswaram is widely played at South Indian temple ceremonies and weddings.

31.correct: The 'Tur' is a cylindrical clay instrument with leather membranes on both sides, a type of drum (dhol/percussion).

wrong.A: It is not a harmonium (a keyboard wind instrument).
wrong.C: It is not a pair of cymbals.

31.fact: Two-sided drums are common in Indian folk and tribal music.

32.correct: The Shalimar Bagh in Jammu and Kashmir (Srinagar) was built by the Mughal emperor Jahangir.
32.fact: Jahangir built the Shalimar Bagh for his wife Nur Jahan; it is a famous Mughal garden.

33.correct: Sachin Nag, an Asian Games gold medallist, was associated with swimming.

wrong.D: He was not a hockey player.

33.fact: Sachin Nag won India's first swimming gold at the 1951 Asian Games.

34.correct: Among the items, the cricket bat (II) is mainly made of wood (willow), so 'Only II' is correct.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong; the helmet is not chiefly wood.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong because the helmet is not wood.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since the bat is wooden.

34.fact: Cricket bats are traditionally made from willow wood for its strength and light weight.

35.correct: Rodents (II) are a biotic (living) factor responsible for grain loss in storage; unsuitable moisture (I) is an abiotic factor - so 'Only II' is the biotic example.

wrong.A: 'Only I' (moisture) is an abiotic factor, not biotic.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since moisture is abiotic.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since rodents are biotic.

35.fact: Biotic factors causing storage losses include insects, rodents, fungi and bacteria.

36.correct: Both statements about poultry farming are correct - it involves rearing domestic fowl (I), and poultry production covers both egg production and broiler (meat) production (II).

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are correct.

36.fact: Layer birds are reared for eggs, while broilers are reared for meat.

37.correct: The primary function of the plant cell wall is to provide structural strength and rigidity to the plant.

wrong.B: It does not directly aid cell division.
wrong.D: Photosynthesis is done by chloroplasts, not the cell wall.

37.fact: The plant cell wall is made mainly of cellulose.

38.correct: The Municipal Performance Index is related to the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.

wrong.B: It is not under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
wrong.C: It is not under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
wrong.D: It is not under the Ministry of Jal Shakti.

38.fact: The index assesses the performance of municipalities/urban local bodies.

39.correct: Avinash Sable won a gold medal in the 3000 m steeplechase at the Asian Games 2023.

wrong.A: He is not a badminton player.

39.fact: Avinash Sable holds the Indian national record in the 3000 m steeplechase.

40.correct: The Human Development Index is published by the UNDP, NOT by the World Economic Forum.

wrong.A: The Global Gender Gap Index is published by the WEF.
wrong.B: The Energy Transition Index is published by the WEF.
wrong.C: The Global Competitiveness Index is published by the WEF.

40.fact: The WEF publishes several indices, but the HDI is a UNDP publication.
